operation: First, create a base cylinder by drawing a 10mm radius circle on the XY workplane and extruding it 5mm along the positive Z-axis. Next, select the top Z face of the base cylinder, draw a 6-sided regular polygon with 8mm diameter, and extrude this polygon 4mm further along the Z-axis. Then, select the top Z face of the extruded hexagon, create a countersink hole with a 3.5mm inner hole diameter, 7.0mm countersink diameter, 2 degree countersink angle, and total hole depth of 6mm. Finally, apply a 1.5mm radius fillet to all edges that are parallel to the Z-axis.
geometry: This model is a two-tiered concentric part: the lower section is a short wide cylinder, while the upper section is a smaller regular hexagonal prism stacked exactly on the center of the cylinder's top face. A centered countersunk hole runs vertically through the full height of both sections, and all vertical standing edges of both the cylinder and hexagonal prism are rounded with a small consistent fillet.
function: This is a hexagonal-head fastener spacer/adapter, designed to be secured with a flat-head countersunk screw, with the hexagonal profile allowing easy tool tightening for use as a standoff, spacing shim, or small adjustment knob base in mechanical assemblies.
